definition: A system that transforms programs from one representation into another.
A system that executes a program by evaluating a representation directly.
A model describing how programs become running behavior.
A component that turns characters into tokens.
A component that turns tokens into syntax structure.
A structured representation of program meaning.
Analysis of names scopes types bindings and constraints.
Verification that operations match type rules.
A compiler-friendly form used for analysis optimization or code generation.
Portable instructions executed by a virtual machine.
A software execution environment for bytecode or managed programs.
Services for memory errors concurrency I/O and dynamic behavior.
A component that connects compiled artifacts and symbols.
A component that places executable artifacts into memory.
Compilation during execution using runtime information.
Translation from one high-level language to another.
A restricted environment for running untrusted or controlled code.
A build process designed to produce traceable repeatable artifacts.
